subject: Science
question_text: Boojho and Paheli were given one mirror each by their teacher. Boojho found his image to be erect and of the same size whereas Paheli found her image erect and smaller in size. This means that the mirrors of Boojho and Paheli are, respectively
answer_key: plane mirror and convex mirror.
answer_explanation: A plane mirror ALWAYS forms an erect image of the same size. A convex mirror ALWAYS forms an erect and diminished (smaller) image.

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following can be used to form a real image?
answer_key: Concave mirror only.
answer_explanation: Of the mirrors listed, only a concave mirror can form a real image (which can be caught on a screen). Plane and convex mirrors only form virtual images.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following can be used to form a real image?

subject: Science
question_text: If an object is placed at a distance of 0.5 m in front of a plane mirror, the distance between the object and the image formed by the mirror will be
answer_key: 1 m
answer_explanation: In a plane mirror, the image is formed at the same distance behind the mirror as the object is in front of it. So, object to mirror = 0.5m, mirror to image = 0.5m. Total distance = 1m.

subject: Science
question_text: You are provided with a concave mirror, a convex mirror, a concave lens and a convex lens. To obtain an enlarged image of an object you can use either
answer_key: concave mirror or convex lens.
answer_explanation: Both a concave mirror and a convex lens are capable of producing enlarged images of an object, depending on the object's position.

subject: Science
question_text: A rainbow can be seen in the sky
options: when the sun is in front of you.
when the sun is behind you.
only at the time of sun rise.
answer_key: when the sun is behind you.
answer_explanation: To see a rainbow, the sun must be shining behind you, and the rain clouds must be in front of you.

subject: Science
question_text: An erect and enlarged image can be formed by
answer_key: only a concave mirror.
answer_explanation: A concave mirror can form an erect and enlarged (virtual) image when the object is placed very close to it. A convex mirror always forms a diminished image.

subject: Science
question_text: You are provided with a convex mirror, a concave mirror, a convex lens and a concave lens. You can get an inverted image from
options: both concave lens and convex lens.
both concave mirror and convex mirror.
both concave mirror and convex lens.
both convex mirror and concave lens.
answer_key: both concave mirror and convex lens.
answer_explanation: A concave mirror and a convex lens can both form real and inverted images.

subject: Science
question_text: An image formed by a lens is erect. Such an image could be formed by a
options: convex lens provided the image is smaller than object.
concave lens provided the image is smaller than object.
concave lens provided the image is larger than object.
concave lens provided the image is of the same size.
answer_key: concave lens provided the image is smaller than object.
answer_explanation: A concave lens always forms a virtual, erect, and diminished (smaller) image.

subject: Science
question_text:
Fill in the blanks:
(a) The inner surface of a steel spoon acts as a ___________ mirror.
(b) The outer surface of a flat steel plate acts as a ___________ mirror.
(c) The outer shining surface of a round bottom steel bowl acts as a __________ mirror.
(d) The inner surface of the reflector of a torch acts as a __________ mirror.
answer_key:
(a) concave
(b) plane
(c) convex
(d) concave
answer_explanation: Relating daily objects to mirror types.

subject: Science
question_text:
State whether the following statements are True or False.
(a) A concave lens can be used to produce an enlarged and erect image.
(b) A convex lens always produces a real image.
(c) The sides of an object and its image formed by a concave mirror are always interchanged.
(d) An object can be seen only if it emits light.
answer_key:
(a) False (A concave lens always produces a diminished image)
(b) False (It produces a virtual image when the object is very close)
(c) True (Lateral inversion occurs in all mirrors)
(d) False (We can see objects if they reflect light as well).
answer_explanation: Evaluating properties of light and lenses.

(a) A concave lens can be used to produce an enlarged and erec

subject: Science
question_text: What type of mirror is used as a side mirror in a scooter? Why is this type of mirror chosen?
answer_key: A convex mirror is used. It forms erect and diminished images, allowing the driver to see a much larger rear area compared to a plane or concave mirror.
answer_explanation: Real-world application of convex mirrors.
provenance_excerpt: What type of mirror is used as a side mirror in a scooter? Why is this type of mirror chosen?

subject: Science
question_text: Observe the figures given as Figure 15.1 carefully. The given figures show the path of light through lenses of two different types, represented by rectangular boxes A and B. What is the nature of lenses A and B?
answer_key: In figure A, parallel rays are converging, so lens A is a convex lens. In figure B, parallel rays are diverging, so lens B is a concave lens.
answer_explanation: Visualizing ray diagrams through lenses.

subject: Science
question_text: Boojho made light from a laser torch to fall on a prism. Will he be able to observe a band of seven colours? Explain with a reason.
answer_key: No, he will not observe a band of seven colours. A laser torch emits monochromatic light (light of a single colour), not white light. Therefore, it cannot undergo dispersion into a spectrum.
answer_explanation: Understanding dispersion and monochromatic light.

question_text: State the correct sequence (1-7) of colours in the spectrum formed by the prisms A and B, shown in Figure 15.2.
answer_key: The colours form a spectrum (VIBGYOR). The bottom-most refracted ray is Violet, and the top-most is Red. The exact sequence must be provided based on the prism orientation and standard VIBGYOR pattern.
answer_explanation: Knowledge of the spectrum.

subject: Science
question_text: The side mirror of a scooter got broken. The mechanic replaced it with a plane mirror. Mention any inconvenience that the driver of the scooter will face while using it?
answer_key: The driver will not be able to see the traffic spread over a large area behind them, as the field of view of a plane mirror is much smaller than that of the original convex mirror.
answer_explanation: Practical consequences of incorrect mirror usage.
provenance_excerpt: The side mirror of a scooter got broken. The mechanic replaced it with a plane mirror. Mention any inconvenience that th

subject: Science
question_text: The concave reflecting surface of a torch got rusted. What effect would this have on the beam of light from the torch?
answer_key: The rusted surface will become diffused and lose its smoothness, greatly reducing its ability to reflect light properly. The beam of the torch will become dim and diffused rather than sharp and focused.
answer_explanation: Effect of reflection surface quality.
provenance_excerpt: The concave reflecting surface of a torch got rusted. What effect would this have on the beam of light from the torch?

subject: Science
question_text: An erect and enlarged image of an object is formed on a screen. Explain how this could be possible.
answer_key: This is a trick question. An erect and enlarged image formed on a screen must be a real image (since it's on a screen). But real images formed by a single lens or mirror are always inverted. So, an erect, enlarged image on a screen requires a combination of lenses, not a single one.
answer_explanation: Critically evaluating image properties.
provenance_excerpt: An erect and enlarged image of an object is formed on a screen. Explain how this could be possible.

subject: Science
question_text: Two different type of lenses are placed on a sheet of newspaper. How will you identify them without touching?
answer_key: By looking at the letters of the newspaper through them. If the letters appear enlarged, it is a convex lens. If the letters appear diminished (smaller), it is a concave lens.
answer_explanation: Practical identification of lenses.
provenance_excerpt: Two different type of lenses are placed on a sheet of newspaper. How will you identify them without touching?

subject: Science
question_text: A shopkeeper wanted to fix a mirror which will give a maximum view of his shop. What type of mirror should he use? Give reason.
answer_key: He should use a convex mirror. Convex mirrors bulge outward and form diminished, erect images, giving them a much wider field of view than plane or concave mirrors.
answer_explanation: Applying knowledge of mirror field of view.

subject: Science
question_text: The distance between an object and a convex lens is changing. It is noticed that the size of the image formed on a screen is decreasing. Is the object moving in a direction towards the lens or away from it?
answer_key: The object is moving away from the lens. In a convex lens, as the object moves further away from the lens (from focus to infinity), the real image formed gets smaller.
answer_explanation: Relationship between object distance and image size.

subject: Science
question_text: Suppose we wish to obtain the real image of a distant tree. Explain two possible ways in which we can do it.
answer_key:
1. Using a concave mirror and a screen: We point the concave mirror towards the distant tree and adjust the screen in front of it to catch the real, inverted, diminished image. 
2. Using a convex lens and a screen: We hold the convex lens facing the tree and place a screen behind it, adjusting the distance to obtain a sharp, real, inverted image.
answer_explanation: Applying optical devices to form images.
provenance_excerpt: Suppose we wish to obtain the real image of a distant tree. Explain two possible ways in which we can do it.

subject: Science
question_text: It was observed that when the distance between an object and a lens decreases, the size of the image increases. What is the nature of this lens? If you keep on decreasing the distance between the object and the lens, will you still able to obtain the image on the screen? Explain.
answer_key: The lens is a convex lens. If you keep decreasing the distance, eventually the object will come very close to the lens. At this point, the lens will form a virtual, erect, and magnified image, which cannot be obtained on the screen.
answer_explanation: Detailed behavior of convex lenses.

subject: Science
question_text: You are given three mirrors of different types. How will you identify each one of them?
answer_key:
By looking at the images formed by them.
1. If the image is erect, virtual, and of the exact same size for all distances, it is a Plane Mirror.
2. If the image is erect and smaller (diminished) for all distances, it is a Convex Mirror.
3. If the image is erect and magnified when the mirror is held close, but becomes inverted when moved away, it is a Concave Mirror.
answer_explanation: Comprehensive test to distinguish mirrors.
provenance_excerpt: You are given three mirrors of different types. How will you identify each one of them?
